title: Building Good Habits That Actually Stick
content:
Most people fail at building new habits not because they lack willpower, but because they rely on willpower alone. Willpower is a finite resource. Good systems make good behavior the path of least resistance.

Start smaller than you think you need to. If you want to exercise daily, start with five minutes. If you want to read more, start with one page. The goal at first is simply to show up — to build the identity of someone who does the thing.

Attach new habits to existing ones. Want to meditate? Do it right after you pour your morning coffee. Want to journal? Do it right before bed. This technique, called habit stacking, uses established routines as anchors.

Track your progress visibly. A simple calendar where you cross off each day you complete your habit creates a chain you won't want to break. Don't break the chain.

Expect to miss a day. The rule to remember: never miss twice. One missed day is an accident. Two missed days is the start of a new habit — the habit of not doing the thing.
